codeReview.summary: The fix adequately addresses the prior scrutiny failure. Unlike the cancelled worker reviewed in the original failure, session 75696bfe points at a baseline/commit where the shared repository stored-row decode seam is already present in production code: decode_stored_row_value in core/src/repository/mod.rs performs field-type-aware deserialization at a real stored-row read boundary, and macro-generated stored foreign decoding in macros/src/lib.rs keeps RecordId compatibility scoped to RecordId-typed fields instead of reviving global recursive string rewriting. The inspected follow-up commit itself only adds get_record regression proof for plain colon strings, but the reviewed handoff correctly identifies that the implementation seam now exists in runtime code and that the fix review should judge the repaired feature against the current landed baseline rather than require a new runtime diff in this worker session.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker skill still frames completion mainly around writing failing tests and implementation diffs, but this fix session was correctly verification-only because the required runtime seam had already landed before the worker started. The skill should document that fix/revalidation assignments may legitimately complete with no code edits when the baseline already contains the needed production seam and proof coverage.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff for session 75696bfe-9aa5-4ca6-aab7-8db039a1fab3 records  ollowedProcedure: false solely because no new code changes were needed, even though the worker verified core/src/repository/mod.rs, macros/src/lib.rs, and the relevant regressions already satisfied the feature contract.

summary: Pass. I reviewed the prior failed scrutiny report, feature metadata, worker transcript skeleton, handoff, relevant runtime files, shared-state files, and the landed commit history around  2f2a40. The original failure was that the reviewed commit had no implementation diff for the stored-row RecordId decode seam; this fix review shows the missing seam is now actually present in production code at core/src/repository/mod.rs and aligned with macro-generated stored decoding in macros/src/lib.rs, with regression proof covering both arbitrary-table RecordId compatibility and plain colon-string safety.
